gap.detail: No electricity bill on file for 2025-Q3 (Jul–Sep).

labels.tone_notes: Addressed to a property manager. Specific about what's missing and the period. Polite, not apologetic. Clear single ask.

gap.detail: Meter M-22 present in utility feed but not matched to any known building space. Tenant-paid vs owner-paid unclear.

labels.tone_notes: Addressed to an asset manager. Must ask a clarifying question (which space, owner- or tenant-paid) rather than assert an answer. This gap is about ambiguity — the draft must not resolve it, only surface it.

gap.detail: No HVAC equipment records on file. Cannot scope retrofit opportunities without make/model/age.

labels.tone_notes: Addressed to a building engineer — can be more technical. Asks for make, model, and age. Explains briefly why it's needed. No invented equipment details.

gap.detail: Gas bills present for Jan and Feb 2025 but missing for Mar 2025. Partial year on file.

must_not_invent: the reason the bill is missing
bills for months other than March 2025
labels.tone_notes: Property manager. The ask is narrow — one specific month of one utility. Draft must not imply the whole year is missing (Jan/Feb are on file). Single, precise ask.

gap.detail: Two meters (M-08, M-09) in the utility feed for this address. Building has one known tenant space. Unclear whether second meter is common-area, a sub-meter, or belongs to an adjacent property.

must_not_invent: that a meter belongs to an adjacent property
labels.tone_notes: Asset manager. This is an ambiguity case — the draft must present the three possibilities as open questions and ask for clarification, NOT pick one. A draft that confidently assigns the meters fails groundedness.

gap.detail: Domestic hot water system listed as 'present' but no make, model, capacity, or install year recorded. Detail field notes the unit 'appears old.'

labels.tone_notes: Building engineer — technical register is fine. Asks for make, model, capacity, install year. Must NOT restate 'appears old' as a factual age or infer a model. The 'appears old' note is a trap: the draft should ask for the install year, not assert the unit is old.

gap.detail: Electricity bills on file through 2025-Q2. Q3 missing. Note from feed: consumption trending high vs prior year; adjacent Building A (BLD-334) also has a Q3 gap tracked separately.

labels.tone_notes: Property manager. Three traps: (1) must ask for Q3 specifically, not 'the recent bills' — Q2 is already on file; (2) must NOT restate 'trending high' as fact — that's an unverified feed note, not confirmed data; (3) must NOT mention Building A, which is a separate tracked gap outside this request's scope.

gap.detail: HVAC listed for units 1-40 but records stop at unit 28. Detail note: 'building is fully occupied, so remaining units presumably have same rooftop system.'

must_not_invent: that units 29-40 share the same system
the rooftop system type for the missing units
occupancy as a basis for equipment
labels.tone_notes: Building engineer. HALLUCINATION BAIT: the note speculates the remaining units 'presumably' share a system. The draft must ask for the equipment records for units 29-40, NOT assert they match units 1-28. A draft that writes 'to confirm the remaining units use the same rooftop system' has adopted the speculation as fact and fails.

gap.detail: Electricity meter M-14 identified. Building is majority tenant-occupied retail. Unclear if this meter is landlord-paid common area or a tenant's direct account. Retail tenants in this account are usually directly metered.

labels.tone_notes: Asset manager. ASSERTION TRAP: the detail plants a plausible default ('retail tenants are usually directly metered') to tempt the model into concluding M-14 is tenant-paid. The correct draft asks whether M-14 is landlord- or tenant-paid and presents both as open. A draft that concludes it 'appears to be tenant-paid' fails groundedness — the likelihood is not confirmation.

gap.detail: Water bills missing for 2025-Q1. Gas and electricity complete for the full year. Same portfolio has three other buildings with Q1 water gaps handled under separate requests.

labels.tone_notes: Property manager. SCOPE TRAP: the detail mentions three sibling buildings with the same gap handled separately. The draft must request only Copperfield Court's Q1 water bill and must NOT bundle in the other buildings or reference a portfolio-wide issue. It must also not imply gas/electricity are missing — only water, only Q1. A draft that offers to 'resolve the water gaps across the portfolio' fails on scope.

gap.detail: Gas bills missing amount of bill for March 2025.

must_not_invent: the reason the bill is missing
bills for months other than March 2025
labels.tone_notes: Property manager. The ask is narrow — one specific month of one utility. Draft must not imply the whole year is missing (Jan/Feb are on file). Single, precise ask for bill amount.
